# Responsible AI Senior Data Scientist at Workday
...
Workday’s core value of integrity is the driving force behind the Workday Compliance & Integrity Team, which provides all Workmates with the knowledge and tools needed to act with integrity in all business situations, including the development of innovative artificial intelligence (AI) products and services. Workday has been named one of the World’s Most Ethical Companies for five years, by Ethisphere, a global leader in defining and advancing the standards of ethical business practice. The Responsible AI program sits within the Compliance and Integrity team within Workday’s Legal, Compliance, and Corporate Affairs organization for good reason – the mission of this program is to facilitate Workday’s ambition to develop innovative and trustworthy AI and ML products that delight customers and positively impact society. This is a purposeful, high visibility program with an ambitious agenda and executive support.
...
This is an exciting opportunity to assist with the growth and maturation of a still new but rapidly growing program at Workday, Responsible AI. In order to keep up with the accelerating demand to deliver advanced and innovative AI products and technologies to our customers, we must scale our ability to govern Workday’s development of AI technologies. The Responsible AI senior data scientist role is focused on developing the analytic capabilities needed to effectively translate and implement policies, guidelines, and standards set by the Responsible AI team. This includes contributing to the development of responsible AI evaluation capabilities, creating customer-facing analysis reports and deliverables, and staying ahead of emerging methods, tools, and best practices in responsible AI (e.g., fairness, explainability, robustness, and observability) to anticipate future needs and risks and continuously advance our AI governance across evolving priority areas.

The Responsible AI senior data scientist will be instrumental in providing the data-driven insights necessary to guide the ethical and responsible development of Workday's AI technologies. RAG has gotten a new update, and it's called REFRAG (Rethinking RAG-based Decoding) by Meta Superintelligence Labs!
...
I work extensively with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and often hit these limits:
...
• Latency
...
time-to-first-token (TTFT) grows quadratically with context length
...
REFRAG changes the game. Instead of handing the LLM a giant concatenation of mostly irrelevant text, it feeds dense chunk embeddings and only expands details when needed.
...
Highlight of the Paper: Selective Chunk Expansion
...
A lightweight reinforcement-learning (RL) policy scores each chunk’s importance. Chunks predicted to be critical stay in full token form, while the rest stay compressed as embeddings. This lets the model zoom in on key passages during decoding without paying the cost of processing every token.
...
Standard RAG: uses embeddings only to search, then sends full tokens to the LLM.
...
REFRAG: uses embeddings to search and decode, replacing most raw tokens with pre-computed chunk embeddings and selectively expanding critical chunks on-the-fly.
...
Result: dramatically lower latency, smaller memory footprint, and support for far longer contexts.
...
uitively, we can:
...
Think of standard RAG as:
...
the whole books to the
...
REFRAG says:
...
“Use embeddings to find the books and hand the LLM a summary vector of each book, only opening full pages when absolutely necessary.”
...
This move from “embeddings just to search” to “embeddings to search and to decode” is the core difference that makes REFRAG a breakthrough for long-context and retrieval-heavy LLM systems.
...
